name: Proto-Turkic (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Turkic is the reconstructed common ancestor of the Turkic family (Oghuz, Kipchak, Karluk, Siberian and the divergent Oghur branch, e.g. Chuvash). It exists here only so that the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern Turkic varieties have a top-level node to resolve their PARENT chain to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / gn.json stub pattern). Glottolog top node turk1311.

sources.author: Johanson, L. & Csató, É. Á. (eds.)
sources.title: The Turkic Languages
sources.notes: Reference for the Turkic family and its internal branching (structural ancestry node only).
